mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate has risen steadily to 4.94% approaching the presumed terminal rate of the 2004-2006 tightening cycle. As FEDFUNDS increases decelerate month over month, markets price in an approaching pause, compressing equity risk premia and lifting SPX over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS reached 4.94% in May 2006 after 16 consecutive hikes since 2004
regime_hint: late-stage monetary tightening cycle nearing terminal rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month deceleration in FEDFUNDS increases and forward 20d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns are not significantly higher when hike pace slows versus when it accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) have declined from a cyclical peak near 2000k units toward 1821k, reflecting cooling residential investment. A sustained drop of more than 10% from the trailing 12-month peak signals a broader demand slowdown that precedes weaker consumer and corporate earnings, dragging SPX lower over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 12-month peak
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 1821.0 down from earlier peaks above 2000 in 2005
regime_hint: housing market downturn following a multi-year boom
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following a >10% HOUST decline from peak are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises faster than CPI inflation, the implied real policy rate climbs, tightening financial conditions beyond what inflation alone would justify. Joint acceleration of FEDFUNDS with stable or falling CPIAUCSL growth signals restrictive real rates that compress equity valuations over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.94% versus CPIAUCSL YoY growth near 3.4% in early 2006
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS minus trailing CPIAUCSL YoY inflation) shows Rank IC below 0.03 against forward 20d SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window, or real-rate increases coincide with positive equity returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment near a cyclical low of 4.6% combined with continued payroll growth indicates a tight labor market late in the expansion, raising wage-inflation risk and the probability of continued Fed tightening. In this late-cycle regime, tight labor markets predict weaker forward equity returns over the following quarter as monetary policy remains restrictive.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.0% while PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% in May 2006 near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns during periods of UNRATE below 5.0% and strong payroll growth are positive in more than 60% of instances, invalidating the late-cycle drag mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals ongoing income and consumption support, which sustains near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces modestly positive SPX returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS rose from 135106 to 136249 thousand between vintage and revision
regime_hint: any regime with positive employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS growth and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS prints are not followed by above-median 5d returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth exceeds 3%, markets anticipate additional Federal Reserve rate hikes beyond current expectations, raising discount rates and producing negative SPX forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value 201.9 implies YoY inflation above 3% in early-mid 2006
regime_hint: elevated inflation regime with active Fed tightening bias
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following CPI YoY prints above 3% are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS above 4.5%) jointly signal that tight credit conditions are directly suppressing housing-driven demand, a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown. This interaction predicts negative SPX returns over the following quarter beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% while HOUST declines for three consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.94% coincides with HOUST declining to 1821k in April 2006
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS interaction signal shows Rank IC below 0.03 against forward 60d SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or triggered instances show positive returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Continued month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy supply-side momentum and corporate output expansion, supporting near-term equity risk appetite and producing positive SPX returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 112.0991 reflects continued industrial expansion as of May 2006
regime_hint: any regime with positive industrial production growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly growth and forward 5d SPX return remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth periods do not show above-median forward returns in more than 55% of cases.
